title: Ring of Depth: Neural Scaling & Emergence

L_N_times_L_D.expression: L_N · L_D = AB · N^{-α} · D^{-β} = AB · exp(-α log N - β log D)
L_N_times_L_D.why: Product of power laws = power law in two variables = EML-2
L_N_times_L_D.ring_check: 2⊗2=2: product of scaling laws stays EML-2

joint_scaling.expression: L(N,D) = total loss: still EML-2 (sum of EML-2 terms)

chinchilla_optimal.why: Optimal allocation = power law of compute = EML-2

threshold_event.ring_question: What ring operation maps EML-2 → EML-∞ at N_crit?
threshold_event.answer: TYPE 2 Horizon crossing: the scaling law meets the capability threshold
threshold_event.mechanism: At N_crit, the scaling law L(N) crosses a PHASE TRANSITION boundary. This is NOT EML-2 ⊗ EML-2 = EML-4 (that would predict smooth capability growth). Instead: EML-2 (scaling) → TYPE 2 Horizon → EML-∞ (emergent capability). In ring terms: the multiplication table gives 2⊗2=2, so emergence CANNOT arise from multiplying two scaling laws together. Emergence requires a TYPE 2 (Horizon) or TYPE 3 (Categorification) transition — it is ORTHOGONAL to the ring multiplication structure.

ring_explanation_of_unpredictability.statement: Why can't we predict emergence from scaling laws? Because emergence is NOT in the ring generated by scaling laws. The ring generated by EML-2 scaling laws closes at EML-2 (2⊗2=2). Emergence lives at EML-∞, which is outside the closed EML-2 ring. You need a TYPE 2 or TYPE 3 step to reach EML-∞ — these are NOT derivable from the EML-2 ring operations alone. This is the ring-theoretic explanation of emergence unpredictability.

grokking_transition.ring_analysis: Δd=1 → Δd=2: sequential (+1 step in additive group)
grokking_transition.not_ring_multiplication: Grokking is NOT a ring multiplication: it's +1 in the additive group

double_grokking_hypothesis.question: Can grokking happen twice (EML-2 → EML-3)?
double_grokking_hypothesis.answer: YES — if the network finds the oscillatory structure of the data
double_grokking_hypothesis.example: Learning Fourier features after memorizing: EML-1→EML-2→EML-3
double_grokking_hypothesis.implication: Multi-stage grokking = additive depth ascent: 1→2→3 (stopping at EML-3)

ensemble_of_two.why: Geometric mean of power laws = power law: EML-2 preserved

mixture_of_experts.expression: L_MoE = Σ_i w_i L_i (weighted EML-2 combination)
mixture_of_experts.why: Weighted mixture = EML-2 (expectation over experts = EML-2)
mixture_of_experts.ring_check: No depth increase from mixing: EML-2 closed under mixture

scaling_ring.key_insight: Scaling ring closure: EML-2 scaling laws form a CLOSED ring (2⊗2=2). Emergence CANNOT arise from the scaling ring — requires TYPE 2 or TYPE 3. This explains emergence unpredictability: it's outside the EML-2 closure.

key_theorem: The Scaling Ring Closure Theorem (S254): Neural scaling laws (Chinchilla, Kaplan) form a CLOSED subring of the depth semiring. Every ring operation on EML-2 scaling laws produces an EML-2 result: product of power laws = power law (EML-2); ensemble/mixture = EML-2. EMERGENCE CANNOT ARISE FROM WITHIN THE SCALING RING. The closure of EML-2 under ⊗ means that no ring-theoretic combination of scaling laws can produce emergent capabilities (EML-∞). Emergence requires stepping OUTSIDE the EML-2 subring via TYPE 2 (Horizon) or TYPE 3. This is the ring-theoretic explanation of emergence unpredictability: the predictive power of scaling laws (EML-2 ring) does not extend to emergence because emergence lives in a different class (EML-∞). Grokking (TYPE 1, Δd=+1): additive ascent, not ring multiplication. Multi-stage grokking 1→2→3 is possible via repeated Δd=+1 additions.
rabbit_hole_log: Scaling laws form closed EML-2 ring: product, ensemble, mixture all stay EML-2
Emergence OUTSIDE the scaling ring: cannot be derived from EML-2 operations (ring-closure proof)
Grokking = additive (1→2→3), not multiplicative; multi-stage grokking predicts EML-3 learning
Chinchilla optimal: L*(C)~C^{-κ} = EML-2; ensemble=EML-2; no depth amplification from combining
